chapter: ENG9_8 – A House Is Not a Home
title: A House Is Not a Home – Medium VSAQs

questions.question: Why did the author feel awkward in his first year of high school?
questions.answer: Because he had to start again in a new and bigger school without his old friends.

questions.question: Why did the author visit his old teachers?
questions.answer: Because he missed them and felt comfortable talking to them.

questions.question: What advice did the author’s old teachers give him?
questions.answer: They advised him to participate in school activities to make new friends.

questions.question: What was the author doing when the fire started?
questions.answer: He was sitting at the dining table doing his homework.

questions.question: Why did the author's mother run into the burning house again?
questions.answer: To save memories of her late husband.

questions.question: Why did the firefighter stop the author?
questions.answer: To prevent him from entering the dangerous burning house.

questions.question: What did the firefighters do for the author after the fire?
questions.answer: They wrapped him in a blanket and comforted him.

questions.question: Why did the author cry after the fire was put out?
questions.answer: Because he realised his cat was missing.

questions.question: Why couldn't the author return to his house that night?
questions.answer: Because it was still unsafe.

questions.question: Why did the author have to go to school the next day?
questions.answer: Because his mother insisted he continue his routine.

questions.question: Why did the author feel like an outcast?
questions.answer: Because he had lost everything and looked different from others.

questions.question: Why did the author say he felt like a zombie?
questions.answer: Because he was emotionally numb and confused.

questions.question: What things were saved from the house?
questions.answer: Photo albums and important documents.

questions.question: Why did the author keep visiting the ruins of his house?
questions.answer: Because he hoped to find his missing cat.

questions.question: Why did news of the fire spread quickly in school?
questions.answer: Because people talked about it and teachers informed others.

questions.question: Why did classmates gather around the author at the gym?
questions.answer: To show him the gifts they had collected.

questions.question: What kinds of items did the students give him?
questions.answer: Clothes, notebooks, and school supplies.

questions.question: How did the classmates’ help affect the author?
questions.answer: It made him feel accepted and hopeful.

questions.question: Why did the author realise his life was being rebuilt?
questions.answer: Because he had new friends and support.

questions.question: How was the cat finally found?
questions.answer: A woman found it and tried to locate the owner.

questions.question: Why had the cat run away during the fire?
questions.answer: Because it was frightened.

questions.question: Why did the woman return the cat?
questions.answer: Because she knew it was loved and missed.

questions.question: What feelings did the author experience after getting the cat back?
questions.answer: Happiness and gratitude.

questions.question: What helped the author overcome his loneliness?
questions.answer: Kindness and friendship from his classmates.

questions.question: What does the story show about human kindness?
questions.answer: Kindness can change someone’s life.
